registry.description: Greed is the insatiable desire to have more — specifically more than one's share, more than one needs, more at others' expense. The Greek terms here cover the taking by force and the consuming hunger for more. Scripture treats greed as a spiritual disorder: it is idolatry (Colossians 3:5) because it displaces God with the pursuit of wealth and security. The greedy person is one whose inner life is perpetually anxious, never resting in what they have, always reaching for what they do not.

terms.medium_def:
greediness, avarice 
some advantage which one possesses over another; an inordinate desire of riches, covetousness, avarice Lk. 12:15; grasping, overreaching, extortion, Rom. 1:29; 1Thess. 2:5; a gift exacted by importunity and conferred with grudging, a hard-wrung gift, 2Cor. 9:5; a scheme of extortion, Mk. 7:22
terms.lsj_entry:
πλεονεξία, 
Ionic dialect -ιη, ἡ, greediness, assumption, arrogance, τῶν Σπαρτιητέων ἡ π. [Refs 5th c.BC+]; π. συγγενική wrong done to one's kin, [Refs 4th c.AD+]
__2 assumption, αἱ ἐν τῷ πυνθάνεσθαι π. [Refs 4th c.BC+]
__II advantage, [Refs 5th c.BC+]; αἱ π. αἱ ἴδιαι, αἱ δημόσιαι, [Refs 5th c.BC+]; ἐπὶ πλεονεξίᾳ with a view to one's own advantage, [Refs 5th c.BC+]; μετὰ πλεονεξίας τινὸς ἀγωνίζεσθαι πρὸς [τὰ θηρία] [Refs]excellences, [Refs 3rd c.AD+]
__II.2 a larger share of a thing, τῶν πολιτικῶν δικαίων [Refs 4th c.BC+]
__II.3 gain derived from a thing, τὴν ἐπὶ τῶν ἰδίων δικῶν πλεονεξίαν [Refs 4th c.BC+]undue gains, [Refs 5th c.BC+]
__III excess, opposed to ἔνδεια, [Refs 5th c.BC+]

terms.medium_def:
greedy person 
one who has, or claims to have more than his share; a covetous, avaricious person, one who defrauds for the sake of gain, 1Cor. 5:10, 11; 6:10; Eph. 5:5*
terms.lsj_entry:
πλεονέκτ-ης, ου, ὁ, 
={ὁ πλέον ἔχων}, one who has or claims more than his due, greedy, grasping, [Refs 5th c.BC+] adjective λόγος π. a greedy, arrogant speech, [Refs 5th c.BC+]: superlative πλεονεκτίστατος [Refs 5th c.BC+]
__2 ἐν παντὶ πλεονέκτην τῶν πολεμίων making gain from their losses, [Refs]
__3 metaphorically in in Mathematics texts, of τὸ ὑπερτελές, [Refs 4th c.AD+]

1f) seat of emotions and passions

terms.medium_def:
1) soul, self, life, creature, person, appetite, mind, living being, desire, emotion, passion
1a) that which breathes, the breathing substance or being, soul, the inner being of man
1b) living being
1c) living being (with life in the blood)
1d) the man himself, self, person or individual
1e) seat of the appetites
1f) seat of emotions and passions
1g) activity of mind
1g1) uncertain
1h) activity of the will
1h1) uncertain
1i) activity of the character
1i1) uncertain
